county_metadata.parent_county: St. Matthew and St. Philip Parishes
county_metadata.record_losses: Early colonial records scattered; fire losses
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1777 from St. Matthew and St. Philip Parishes.
county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Springfield. Original 1777 county — colonial-era records from St. Matthew and St. Philip Parishes. Salzburger heritage (German Protestant settlers at Ebenezer, 1734). GA Salzburger Society records. Jerusalem Evangelical Lutheran Church records among GA's oldest. Early records scattered. Effingham County Historical Society.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Colonial-era parish registers, vestry minutes, and related records. Coverage varies by parish.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Tax digests often survive when other records are lost — many at GA Archives on microfilm

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Elberton. Created 1790 from Wilkes. Elbert County Historical Society. 'Granite Capital of the World' — quarry records document many workers. GA Guidestones (1980-2022) site. Richard B. Russell Dam and Lake records. FamilySearch microfilm. Revolutionary War-era land grants.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Swainsboro. 1855 courthouse fire destroyed early records. Created 1812 from Bulloch and Montgomery. Emanuel County Historical Society. East central GA agricultural county. FamilySearch microfilm from 1855 restart. GA Archives for surviving copies.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Claxton. Created 1914 from Bulloch and Tattnall. 'Fruitcake Capital of the World' (Claxton Bakery). Small county — check parent counties for pre-1914 records. Evans County Historical Society. FamilySearch microfilm.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Blue Ridge. Created 1854 from Gilmer and Union. North GA mountain county — Appalachian heritage. Copper mining records (Copperhill area nearby). Fannin County Heritage Foundation. Cherokee removal area. FamilySearch microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Cherokee Nation Records — Fannin County Area
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-removal Cherokee records including census rolls, treaty records, and land lottery entries. Cherokee removal completed by 1838.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Fayetteville. Created 1821 from Creek Cession lands. Fayette County Historical Society. Southern piedmont county. Atlanta metro growth area. FamilySearch microfilm. Margaret Mitchell connection (Jonesboro area). Church records supplement courthouse records.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1864 Civil War destruction; partial records lost
county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Rome. 1864 Civil War destruction — partial records lost. Created 1832 from Cherokee Cession. Rome is 'City of Seven Hills.' Chieftains Museum/Major Ridge Home (Cherokee history). Sara Hightower Regional Library has genealogy collections. Rome Area History Museum. Berry College archives. Significant Cherokee removal records.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Cumming. Created 1832 from Cherokee Cession. Forsyth County Historical Society. Cherokee removal history. Lake Lanier (1957) affected land records and cemeteries. North GA mountain/piedmont transition. Rapid suburban growth. FamilySearch microfilm.

county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Carnesville. Early records lost in multiple fires. Created 1784 — one of GA's oldest counties. Franklin County Historical Society. Lavonia Carnegie Library has local history collections. Multiple parent county relationships. GA Archives microfilm essential. Revolutionary War-era settlement area.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1864 burning of Atlanta; many records destroyed
county_metadata.research_tips: Clerk of Superior Court in Atlanta. 1864 burning of Atlanta destroyed many records. GA's most populous county. Atlanta History Center has exceptional genealogical collections. Auburn Avenue Research Library on African American Culture and History. Atlanta-Fulton County Library System genealogy department. Fulton absorbed Campbell County (1932) and Milton County (1932) — check those records. City directories from 1859. Extensive African American records.
